commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

issues.description: The forecast items are purely static; adding hover effects or transitions would improve the feel.
issues.fix: Add a subtle hover background color or scale effect to the forecast day containers.

issues.description: Lack of visual hierarchy for the forecast items, as the temperature text size is identical for all.
issues.fix: Use different text weights or colors for the high/low temperatures to provide better distinction.

issues.description: Missing aria-labels for icons to describe current weather status to screen readers.
issues.fix: Add aria-label attributes to the emoji containers to provide descriptive text for the weather condition.

evaluations.critique: The component is clean, well-structured, and correctly leverages the design system primitives. It successfully meets all data binding requirements, though it could benefit from small stylistic refinements and accessibility enhancements to move from 'good' to 'production-ready'.

evaluation.critique: The component has a solid structure with proper use of ggui primitives and good semantic color tokens, but falls short in several areas: it uses hardcoded size values like '4xl' and '2xl' instead of spacing tokens, lacks hover/focus states and transitions for interactivity, and doesn't handle missing forecast data gracefully (shows 'Day' and '☀️' placeholders rather than rendering nothing).

commit.name: Multi-Step Survey Form
commit.description: Multi-step form with validation, progress tracking, and review
commit.prompt:
Build a multi-step survey form with 4 steps:
- Step 1: Text inputs for name (required) and email (required, must be valid email format) with inline validation
- Step 2: Radio button group for satisfaction rating (1-5 scale with labels from props)
- Step 3: Checkbox group for feature interests (options from props)
- Step 4: Textarea for open-ended comments (optional, with character counter, max 500 chars)

Requirements:
- Progress bar at the top showing current step (e.g., "Step 2 of 4")
- Back/Next navigation buttons (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Final review step showing all answers before submission
- Call props.onSubmit with all collected answers as a structured object
- All option labels and feature lists must come from props
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

featureOptions.description: Available feature options for the checkbox step

satisfactionLabels.description: Labels for satisfaction rating (e.g., Very Unsatisfied to Very Satisfied)

submit.label: Submit Survey
submit.description: Called with all collected answers as a structured object

issues.description: The logic for the progress bar calculation is slightly off (denominator should be 5 instead of 4 to reflect the review step).
issues.fix: Change the progress calculation to ((currentStep - 1) / 4) * 100.

issues.description: The 'Next' button enablement only checks for presence of strings, ignoring the email validation state set in the errors object.
issues.fix: Update the 'Next' button disabled logic to check for the absence of validation errors.

issues.description: Form controls within steps could benefit from explicit fieldset/legend or heading elements for better screen reader context during navigation.
issues.fix: Add a hidden or visible heading inside each step section describing the context.

evaluations.critique: The component is well-structured and follows the design system patterns effectively. It covers all functional requirements including the review step and props handling. A minor improvement to the progress bar logic and button validation state would make the user experience perfectly polished.

evaluation.critique: The component has solid structure and good data handling, but uses some hardcoded pixel values (24px, 16px, 12px, 8px) instead of design tokens for spacing, and the review section uses an inline style for borderRadius instead of a token. The progress calculation is off—it goes to step 5 but progress only calculates through step 4.

commit.name: Kanban Task Board
commit.description: Column-based task board with task cards and state management
commit.prompt:
Build a Kanban-style task board with 3 columns: To Do, In Progress, Done.

Each column:
- Has a header with column name and task count
- Contains a vertical list of task cards
- Has an "Add Task" button at the bottom

Each task card displays:
- Task title (editable on double-click or via edit button)
- Priority badge (low/medium/high with color coding from design tokens)
- Assignee avatar or initials
- Due date (if set)
- A move button or dropdown to move the task to another column

Requirements:
- Tasks can be moved between columns via move controls (buttons or select dropdown)
- New tasks start in "To Do" column
- "Add Task" shows an inline form with title input and priority selector
- Track total tasks and completed count in a summary bar at the top
- All initial tasks come from props as an array with column assignment
- Call props.onTaskUpdate when a task is moved, edited, or created
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors, spacing, and shadows

tasks.description: Array of tasks with id, title, priority (low/medium/high), assignee initials, column ID, and optional dueDate

columns.description: Column definitions: [{id: "todo", name: "To Do"}, {id: "in-progress", name: "In Progress"}, {id: "done", name: "Done"}]

taskUpdate.label: Task Updated
taskUpdate.description: Called when a task is moved, edited, or created. Payload: { action, taskId, data }

issues.description: Select labels inside cards are visible and repetitive, creating redundant screen reader announcements for every task card.
issues.fix: Hide the label visually using a 'sr-only' class or aria-label, and ensure the select has a unique aria-labelledby or aria-label referencing the task title.

issues.description: The double-click editing trigger is not discoverable for non-power users.
issues.fix: Add an edit icon/button alongside the task title to make the edit capability clear.

issues.description: The layout is rigid; horizontal stacking on small screens might cause overflow issues.
issues.fix: Implement a responsive design approach where columns stack vertically on smaller breakpoints.

evaluations.critique: The component is functional, well-structured, and adheres closely to the design system constraints. It successfully implements the core Kanban requirements, though it could benefit from better focus management during editing and more discoverable edit controls.

evaluation.critique: The component has a solid Kanban structure with proper column layout and task filtering, but suffers from inconsistent token usage (hardcoded pixel values like padding={16}, padding={12}) and missing hover/focus states on interactive elements. Data presentation is good with proper props handling, though the edit UI could be more polished.

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

issues.description: Grid cells have fixed minimum widths and no specific aspect ratio, which may cause layout shifting if text wraps or screen size is constrained.
issues.fix: Add aspect-ratio: 1 / 1 to the grid cells to ensure a consistent square appearance for elements.

issues.description: The search functionality hides elements by setting opacity to 0.3 rather than filtering them out of the DOM flow, which can be visually confusing.
issues.fix: Consider setting display: none for elements where isMatch is false to clear the board, or provide clearer feedback.

issues.description: The details panel appears dynamically without an aria-live region, so screen reader users may not notice when the selection changes.
issues.fix: Add aria-live='polite' to the detail card container.

evaluations.critique: The component is well-structured, follows the design system tokens accurately, and handles the periodic grid layout effectively using CSS Grid. It meets all functional requirements; adding slight polish to focus states and screen reader announcements would make it production-grade.

evaluation.critique: The component successfully implements a grid-based periodic table with proper responsive overflow handling and search filtering. Main issues: hardcoded padding values (8px, 4px) should use design tokens, missing hover/focus visual feedback on element cards beyond opacity change, and the detail panel could benefit from better visual separation and transitions.

commit.name: Chat Interface
commit.description: Chat UI with message list, input area, and send callback
commit.prompt:
Build a chat interface component. Layout:

Header:
- Chat title from props
- Participant count badge

Message list:
- Scrollable area showing all messages from props
- Each message shows: sender name, message text, formatted timestamp
- Messages from the current user (where sender matches props.currentUser) align right with primary color background
- Messages from others align left with surfaceVariant background
- Group consecutive messages from same sender

Input area at the bottom:
- Text input field for composing messages
- Send button (disabled when input is empty)
- Call props.onSend with { text, timestamp: new Date().toISOString() } on click or Enter

Requirements:
- All data from props — no hardcoded messages
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing
- Auto-scroll to bottom of message list on mount

currentUser.description: Current user name (for right-aligning own messages)

messages.description: Array of messages with sender name, text, and ISO timestamp

example.text: Hey team, the design review is at 3pm

sendMessage.label: Send Message
sendMessage.description: Called when user sends a message. Payload: { text, timestamp }

messages.text: Hey team, the design review is at 3pm
Got it, I will prepare the mockups
Also, can someone review the color palette?
I pushed the latest changes to the design branch
I will take a look after the meeting

issues.description: Requirement to group consecutive messages from the same sender was ignored.
issues.fix: Implement a logic loop that checks if msg[i].sender === msg[i-1].sender and hides the sender name label or clusters the bubbles visually.

issues.description: Component manages internal state for messages initialized from props, which can lead to synchronization issues if the parent updates the messages prop later.
issues.fix: Derive state from props or use a useEffect to update internal state when props.messages changes, or use the incoming prop directly for rendering.

issues.description: Missing ARIA live region for incoming messages. Screen reader users will not be notified when new messages arrive.
issues.fix: Add aria-live='polite' to the message list container.

evaluations.critique: The component is functional and well-structured, successfully meeting the core layout and styling requirements. However, it fails to implement the specific 'grouping' requirement and has potential bugs regarding state synchronization between props and internal message state.

evaluation.critique: The component has solid layout and design token usage, but lacks visual sender name display (only text/timestamp shown), missing sender name grouping logic mentioned in the prompt, and could use more polish with loading states and transition effects on message delivery.

commit.name: Stock Ticker Dashboard
commit.description: Stock price display with color-coded changes and responsive grid
commit.prompt:
Build a stock ticker dashboard that displays stock prices. Layout:
- Header with "Market Watch" title and a "Last Updated" timestamp from props
- Responsive grid of stock cards (3 per row on desktop, 1 on mobile)
- Each card shows: ticker symbol (large, bold), company name, current price formatted with 2 decimals, change amount, and change percentage
- Color coding: green text/border for positive change, red for negative change
- A subtle arrow icon (▲ or ▼) next to the change percentage

All stock data comes from props. Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing.

stocks.description: Array of stocks with symbol, name, price, change, changePercent

lastUpdated.description: ISO timestamp of last price update

issues.description: The card component lacks a border visual indicator for positive/negative change despite the prompt request.
issues.fix: Add a conditional border property to the Card component styles based on the isPositive boolean.

issues.description: The grid layout uses auto-fit, which makes it harder to guarantee exactly 3 per row as requested.
issues.fix: Update gridTemplateColumns to 'repeat(3, 1fr)' with a media query for mobile to ensure the 3-per-row requirement is strictly met.

issues.description: No feedback or visual state for the 'connecting/updating' status provided in the state logic.
issues.fix: Add a simple loading indicator or visual transition when isConnecting becomes true.

evaluations.critique: The component is very well-structured and uses the design tokens appropriately. It handles the data-to-UI mapping cleanly, though the CSS grid logic could be more prescriptive to strictly enforce the requested layout constraints.

evaluation.critique: The component has solid layout and responsive grid implementation with good use of design tokens, but lacks visual affordances for positive/negative change states (missing border color coding on cards) and could improve interactive polish with hover effects. Some token fallbacks use raw pixel values (24px, 16px, 8px, 4px) which slightly violates token-only principles.

commit.name: Onboarding Wizard
commit.description: Multi-step onboarding flow with step indicator and validation
commit.prompt:
Build a 3-step onboarding wizard. Steps:

Step 1 - Profile Setup:
- Name input (required)
- Email input (required, validate email format)
- Avatar selection (choose from 4 emoji avatars)

Step 2 - Preferences:
- Role selection (dropdown: Developer, Designer, Manager, Other)
- Notification toggle (email notifications on/off)
- Theme preference (light/dark toggle)

Step 3 - Review & Confirm:
- Summary of all selections from steps 1-2
- "Go Back" button to edit
- "Complete Setup" button to submit

Requirements:
- Step indicator at top showing current step (1/2/3) with progress bar
- Back/Next navigation (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Smooth transition between steps
- Call props.onComplete with all collected data on final submit
- All initial values from props (for editing existing profile)
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

initialProfile.description: Pre-filled profile data for editing (all fields optional)

avatarOptions.description: Available avatar emojis to choose from

roleOptions.description: Available role options for the dropdown

complete.label: Complete Setup
complete.description: Called with all profile data when user completes onboarding

issues.description: Lack of visual transition or animation during step changes.
issues.fix: Wrap the step content in a CSS transition component or framer-motion container to smooth out the switching between steps.

issues.description: The review summary uses a plain map for labels, which may not be screen-reader friendly for complex keys.
issues.fix: Use a descriptive dictionary to map keys to proper labels (e.g., 'emailNotifications' -> 'Email Notifications') and ensure proper heading hierarchy.

issues.description: The 'Review' summary text layout is slightly unpolished.
issues.fix: Use a definition list (dl/dt/dd) or a styled grid for key-value pairs to improve readability.

evaluations.critique: The component is well-structured, follows props contracts strictly, and correctly implements validation logic for the first step. The code is clean and modular, though adding simple enter/exit transitions and better formatting for the final summary would elevate it to a professional production standard.

evaluation.critique: The component has a functional layout with proper step progression and validation, but inconsistently uses design tokens (mixes var(--ggui-spacing-*) with hardcoded pixel values like '16' and '12'), and lacks visual polish with missing hover/focus states and transitions. The review step renders all data correctly but could benefit from better formatting and visual distinction between labels and values.
